loungeAccess.conditions: Requires min. ₹20,000 spend in the current month to unlock lounge and golf access for the following month (revised effective 1 April 2026); also includes 1 complimentary golf round/lesson per quarter.

movieBenefit.description: Buy-One-Get-One movie ticket via the District app, discount up to ₹250 on the second ticket

history.oldValue: 10 Reward Points per ₹150 spent (10X tier, on monthly spends above ₹20,000)
history.newValue: 10 Reward Points per ₹200 spent (10X tier, on monthly spends above ₹20,000)

source.name: IDFC FIRST Bank
source.document: Important Update Regarding FIRST Wealth Credit Card

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: Yes, IDFC FIRST Wealth has no joining fee and zero annual fee for life, with no minimum spend condition required to keep the fee waived.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: It's a strong no-fee option for high spenders — you get lounge access, golf, a low 1.5% forex markup, and reward points that don't expire, but only if you consistently cross ₹20,000 in monthly spends; below that threshold the base reward rate is fairly low for a premium card.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Higher-income individuals who spend more than ₹20,000/month and travel internationally, and want a free premium card without an annual fee.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: The standard Visa variant does not support UPI directly, but IDFC FIRST offers a separate RuPay variant of FIRST Wealth (₹199 annual fee) that can be linked to UPI apps for 3X reward points on UPI spends.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Yes — 1 complimentary domestic and 1 international airport lounge visit per quarter, plus a quarterly golf round, but only if you spent at least ₹20,000 in the previous month.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: IDFC FIRST Wealth generally targets high-income applicants, with reported eligibility around ₹36 lakh annual income.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Lower or irregular spenders who won't consistently hit the ₹20,000 monthly threshold will get a low base reward rate and lose lounge/golf access; those wanting a simpler premium card with richer guaranteed benefits may prefer a fee-based alternative.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: HDFC Diners Club Black, HDFC Regalia Gold, or SBI Card Elite offer richer benefits for cardholders willing to pay an annual fee.
